title: Was my interpretation Wrong
wikitext:
[]: Was my interpretation Wrong?             Quote Post by SawItAtAge10 » Wed Sep 02, 2015 9:37 am Okay, so like a day or two ago I was at a vid on YouTube for the song "Emergency Evacuation to Regression" off the End of Evangelion soundtrack. It was in the comment section that I had this conversation: PERSON: Far in the distance is cast a shadow Symbol of our freedom will bring us salvation On the horizon hope for Tomorrow Sweeping across the land to give us unity Look to the heavens with tears of triumph To cherish a new life and suffer not again Lift up your spirits from all destruction Never shall we return from conflict we must learn Hidden Message :3 ME: Almost sounds like the mantra for a death cult akin to Heaven's Gate or Aum Shinrikyo... PERSON: Thanks....? ME: Sorry, didn't mean to be creepy. I just meant that these lyrics (especially if sung as a group - as is evidenced by the operatic voices performing it here) give off a sort of collective "dying" feel to it whilst perpetuating the idea of some kind of "transcendence" (generally of a spiritual nature). Consider this in regards to SEELE and what's going on during this scene plus the latter half of the original NGE series with regards to what was going to in real world such as the Tokyo Subway attacks and things of that sort. The symbolism, thus, becomes all the more clearer especially when you view the world armies invading/attacking NERV because they are the ones being viewed as a terrorists/global death cult of epic proportions. Very provocative and creepy when looked at from this perspective, but hey it's just my interpretation. Thoughts??? Also, what did you mean by "hidden message?"P.S. It would seem other anime also incorporated some kind of reaction to those terrorists...for example look at the "Chapter Black Saga" from Yu Yu Hakusho, a lot of similar themes are present there as well. To everyone reading this, please let me you know what you think about how I interpreted the lyrics in relation to elements from the series. Thanks! :)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Sep 02, 2015 11:11 am "Emergency Evacuation to Regression" is from the scene where Asuka discovers Kyoko inside Eva-02. A longer version of the same track is "Escape to the Beginning", which covers everything from GNR rising up to Shinji melting down in rapture within Eva-01.

[]: Was my interpretation Wrong?             Quote Post by SawItAtAge10 » Wed Sep 02, 2015 9:37 am Okay, so like a day or two ago I was at a vid on YouTube for the song "Emergency Evacuation to Regression" off the End of Evangelion soundtrack. PERSON: Thanks....? ME: Sorry, didn't mean to be creepy. I just meant that these lyrics (especially if sung as a group - as is evidenced by the operatic voices performing it here) give off a sort of collective "dying" feel to it whilst perpetuating the idea of some kind of "transcendence" (generally of a spiritual nature). Consider this in regards to SEELE and what's going on during this scene plus the latter half of the original NGE series with regards to what was going to in real world such as the Tokyo Subway attacks and things of that sort. The symbolism, thus, becomes all the more clearer especially when you view the world armies invading/attacking NERV because they are the ones being viewed as a terrorists/global death cult of epic proportions.
